Методология разделяет пары примеров на согласованные (чистые) и конфликтующие (шумные), обучаясь сначала на чистом подмножестве, а затем итеративно улучшаясь на шумных данных. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что Semi-DPO достигает лучшей выравнивания с комплексными человеческими предпочтениями без дополнительных аннотаций.

en.title: Refining Visual Preferences with Semi-DPO
en.desc: The paper introduces Semi-DPO, a method designed to tackle label noise in multi-dimensional visual preference learning. It identifies that traditional binary labeling of images leads to conflicting signals that hinder the learning process. By treating consistent pairs as clean data and conflicting pairs as noisy data, Semi-DPO refines the learning through a semi-supervised approach. The results show that this method significantly enhances alignment with human preferences while avoiding the need for extra human annotations.

zh.title: Semi-DPO：解决多维视觉偏好中的标签噪声
zh.desc: Semi-DPO是一种半监督学习方法，旨在解决多维视觉偏好学习中的标签噪声问题。它将一致的图像对视为干净的标记数据，而将冲突的图像对视为噪声数据，从而通过迭代优化实现与复杂人类偏好的更好对齐。该方法首先在经过共识过滤的干净子集上进行训练，然后利用该模型为噪声数据生成伪标签，进行迭代精炼。实验结果表明，Semi-DPO在性能上达到了最先进的水平，显著提高了与人类复杂偏好的对齐度。

papers.title: FlashRT: Towards Computationally and Memory Efficient Red-Teaming for Prompt Injection and Knowledge Corruption
papers.abstract: Abstract FlashRT is a framework that significantly improves the efficiency of optimization-based prompt injection and knowledge corruption attacks against long-context large language models, enabling faster and more scalable security evaluations.  AI-generated summary Long-context large language models (LLMs)-for example, Gemini-3.1-Pro and Qwen-3.5-are widely used to empower many real-world applications, such as retrieval-augmented generation, autonomous agents, and AI assistants. However, security remains a major concern for their widespread deployment, with threats such as prompt injection and knowledge corruption. To quantify the security risks faced by LLMs under these threats, the research community has developed heuristic-based and optimization-based red-teaming methods. Optimization-based methods generally produce stronger attacks than heuristic attacks and thus provide a more rigorous assessment of LLM security risks. However, they are often resource-intensive, requiring significant computation and GPU memory, especially for long context scenarios. The resource-intensive nature poses a major obstacle for the community (especially academic researchers) to systematically evaluate the security risks of long-context LLMs and assess the effectiveness of defense strategies at scale. In this work, we propose FlashRT, the first framework to improve the efficiency (in terms of both computation and memory) for optimization-based prompt injection and knowledge corruption attacks under long-context LLMs. Through extensive evaluations, we find that FlashRT consistently delivers a 2x-7x speedup (e.g., reducing runtime from one hour to less than ten minutes) and a 2x-4x reduction in GPU memory consumption (e.g., reducing from 264.1 GB to 65.7 GB GPU memory for a 32K token context) compared to state-of-the-art baseline nanoGCG. FlashRT can be broadly applied to black-box optimization methods, such as TAP and AutoDAN. We hope FlashRT can serve as a red-teaming tool to enable systematic evaluation of long-context LLM security. The code is available at: https://github.com/Wang-Yanting/FlashRT

ru.title: Быстрое красное тестирование для безопасности длинноконтекстных моделей
ru.desc: FlashRT — это фреймворк для ускорения оптимизационных атак на большие языковые модели с длинным контекстом, включая инъекции подсказок и атаки на знания модели. Метод значительно снижает вычислительные затраты и потребление памяти GPU (2-7 раз ускорение, 2-4 раза экономия памяти) по сравнению с существующими подходами. Фреймворк совместим с различными методами чёрного ящика оптимизации и позволяет систематически оценивать уязвимости длинноконтекстных LLM на масштабе. Это делает красное тестирование безопасности доступным для академических исследователей и упрощает разработку защитных механизмов.

en.title: FlashRT: Revolutionizing Security Evaluations for Long-Context LLMs
en.desc: FlashRT is a novel framework designed to enhance the efficiency of optimization-based attacks on long-context large language models (LLMs), specifically targeting prompt injection and knowledge corruption. By optimizing both computation and memory usage, FlashRT achieves significant speed improvements, reducing attack runtime by 2x to 7x and cutting GPU memory requirements by 2x to 4x. This advancement allows researchers to conduct more extensive and systematic security evaluations of LLMs, which are crucial for understanding and mitigating potential vulnerabilities. FlashRT is applicable to various black-box optimization methods, making it a versatile tool for red-teaming efforts in AI security.

zh.title: FlashRT：提升长上下文大语言模型安全评估效率的利器
zh.desc: FlashRT是一个框架，显著提高了针对长上下文大语言模型的优化基础的提示注入和知识腐蚀攻击的效率。该框架通过减少计算和内存消耗，使得安全评估变得更快、更可扩展。研究表明，FlashRT在运行时间上实现了2到7倍的加速，并在GPU内存使用上减少了2到4倍。我们希望FlashRT能够作为红队工具，帮助系统性地评估长上下文大语言模型的安全性。

papers.title: Safety Drift After Fine-Tuning: Evidence from High-Stakes Domains
papers.abstract: Abstract Downstream adaptation of foundation models leads to unpredictable changes in safety behavior, challenging current governance practices that rely on base-model evaluations.  AI-generated summary Foundation models are routinely fine-tuned for use in particular domains, yet safety assessments are typically conducted only on base models, implicitly assuming that safety properties persist through downstream adaptation. We test this assumption by analyzing the safety behavior of 100 models, including widely deployed fine-tunes in the medical and legal domains as well as controlled adaptations of open foundation models alongside their bases. Across general-purpose and domain-specific safety benchmarks, we find that benign fine-tuning induces large, heterogeneous, and often contradictory changes in measured safety: models frequently improve on some instruments while degrading on others, with substantial disagreement across evaluations. These results show that safety behavior is not stable under ordinary downstream adaptation, raising critical questions about governance and deployment practices centered on base-model evaluations. Without explicit re-evaluation of fine-tuned models in deployment-relevant contexts, such approaches fall short of adequately managing downstream risk, overlooking practical sources of harm -- failures that are especially consequential in high-stakes settings and challenge current accountability paradigms.

ru.title: Безопасность моделей нестабильна при адаптации к новым доменам
ru.desc: В работе исследуется, как адаптация фундаментальных моделей к конкретным задачам влияет на их безопасность. Авторы протестировали 100 моделей, включая доменные варианты в медицине и праве, и обнаружили, что fine-tuning вызывает непредсказуемые и противоречивые изменения в поведении моделей. На одних бенчмарках безопасность улучшается, а на других деградирует, что указывает на нестабильность свойств безопасности при адаптации. Результаты показывают, что текущие подходы оценки безопасности только базовых моделей неадекватны для управления рисками в high-stakes приложениях.

en.title: Safety Uncertainty in Fine-Tuned Models
en.desc: This paper investigates how fine-tuning foundation models for specific tasks can lead to unpredictable changes in their safety behavior. The authors analyze 100 models, including those used in sensitive areas like medicine and law, and find that fine-tuning can both improve and degrade safety performance across different benchmarks. Their results indicate that safety properties do not remain consistent after adaptation, which challenges the assumption that base-model evaluations are sufficient for governance. The study highlights the need for re-evaluating fine-tuned models in real-world contexts to effectively manage risks and ensure accountability.

zh.title: 微调模型的安全性评估需重新审视
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了基础模型在下游适应过程中的安全行为变化。研究发现，经过微调的模型在安全性评估中表现不稳定，可能在某些方面有所改善，而在其他方面却出现恶化。作者分析了100个模型，包括医疗和法律领域的微调模型，结果显示安全性评估不能仅依赖基础模型。论文强调需要在实际应用中对微调模型进行重新评估，以有效管理潜在风险。
